vibe_report.summary: A measured, low-key luxury Japandi interior: deep matte walls and smoked oak joinery warmed by brass accents, natural textiles and a restrained plant presence. Spaces feel intimate and textural with layered ambient light; styling is sparse, family-ready and includes a compact standing work corner. Prioritize calm sightlines between living, dining and kitchen; keep material and color consistency across shared objects.
vibe_report.palette_rationale: A warm-neutral palette anchored in charcoal and smoked oak (#0B0B0B / #2F2F2F / Smoked Oak #73503A) provides the dark, graphic base from the Pinterest cluster. Accents in warm brass (#D99A48) and mid-gray upholstery (#8F8F8F) introduce glow and tactile contrast without brightening the scheme. Use one soft warm-neutral rug tone to ground the floor planes.
vibe_report.lighting_mood: Soft ambient with directional window pulls. Primary illumination comes from exterior daylight through bay glazing and a single bedroom window; supplement with warm practicals (aged-brass pendant, brass wall sconces, low-wattage floor lamp) and concealed warm LED cove or ribbed-panel backlight to create an intimate glow. Avoid overhead high-CRI cool white fixtures that break the warm tone.
materials: smoked oak joinery and slatted panels (matte finish)
matte black steel for task furniture
furniture_language: vertical slat backs and open shelving
compact utilitarian standing desk with concealed cable runs
curated single-object displays rather than clusters
styling_rules: one sculptural metallic accent (aged brass) per vignette
layer bedding and cushions in 2–3 tonal steps (light gray -> charcoal -> black)
leave negative space on shelving; use books and 1–2 ceramics per shelf
place a single bonsai or sculptural plant as organic counterpoint in living/dining sightlines
keep visible tech minimal and integrated (monitor arm, cable channel)
avoid: high-gloss finishes that reflect too strongly
overcrowded shelves or heavy decorative sets
mismatched furniture visible across open sightlines

room_guidance.headline: Japandi Noir living hub — low sectional, sculptural coffee, work corner by the bay
room_guidance.guidance: Frame the bay window as the primary light source. Anchor the seating on the neutral woven area rug with the low-profile 3-seater sofa and the black marble coffee table centered. Locate the adjustable standing desk + bookshelf at the bay return to form a calm work corner: keep desk facing the window, bookshelf perpendicular to sightline. Add one floor lamp or recessed slat backlight for evening warmth.
must_include: low-profile 3-seater sofa — charcoal slate linen upholstery (Charcoal Slate #2F2F2F), smoked oak tapered legs, low bench form
black marble coffee table — round monolithic black marble, honed finish (Jet Black #0B0B0B)
neutral woven area rug — low-pile wool blend in warm oat
standing desk — compact adjustable-height standing desk, dark-stained oak top, matte black steel T-leg base
open bookshelf — freestanding smoked oak 5-shelf unit, matte finish
bonsai plant — small rounded ficus bonsai in unglazed ceramic pot
must_avoid: bright table lamps or cool white overheads
large ottomans that block the bay window sightline

room_guidance.headline: Moody, calm bedroom — black tactile backdrop, warm brass bedside accents
room_guidance.guidance: Use matte charcoal walls with a smoked oak vertical-slatted headboard wall. Keep the bed low and centrally composed under the window. Add layered bedding textures and one brass bedside lamp per side with sculptural ceramics on a single floating shelf. Keep styling minimal: one plant and one framed print max.
must_include: vertical smoked oak slat accent (use same Smoked Oak finish as living joinery)
layered bedding in light gray and charcoal
sculptural brass dome bedside lamp (aged warm brass accent)
neutral woven textiles and one small plant
must_avoid: large metallic photo frames that reflect light

room_guidance.headline: Compact warm kitchen — smoked oak accents, matte surfaces, visible prep zone
room_guidance.guidance: Keep cabinetry matte and low-contrast; use smoked oak open shelves and a short run of countertop under the small window. Visible cooking surfaces should be matte black or dark stone; position small practical pendants only over the prep/island if present. Keep dishware simple and neutral, with one small plant or ceramic as the organic touch.
must_include: smoked oak open shelving (matching Smoked Oak finish)
muted dark stone or matte black counter for visible prep
aged warm brass small task sconce or pendant (if pendant over island, match dining pendant finish)

room_guidance.headline: Intimate corner dining — compact smoked oak table, single aged-brass pendant, bonsai vignette
room_guidance.guidance: Place the rectangular smoked oak dining table close to the bay corner window so daylight sculpts the table surface. Hang the aged warm brass dome pendant centered 60cm above table. Keep seating minimal — 4 slim chairs — and use the same neutral woven rug if the sightline benefits from continuity with the living area.
must_include: rectangular smoked oak dining table — matte smoked oak finish (Smoked Oak #73503A)
aged warm brass dome pendant — centered over table
bonsai plant (shared) if visible from living sightline
must_avoid: mismatched dining chairs visible from living room

room_guidance.headline: Quiet micro-bath — soft materials, frosted window, minimal monochrome fixtures
room_guidance.guidance: Keep finishes matte and restrained. Use warm LED mirror strip or brass sconce for task light, derive ambient light only from the frosted window and practical fixtures. Keep towels and accessories in warm neutral tones.
must_include: matte black or dark stone vanity top
warm LED mirror or brass sconce (aged warm brass accent)
neutral towels and single small plant or ceramic

warnings: Dining footprint is small (7.8 x 8 ft): select a compact table (approx 150 cm length) and slim chairs to prevent blocking the living sightline.
Kitchen measured dimensions were not provided — avoid oversized islands or appliances that would violate the open-plan sightline scale.

overall_mood: low-key intimate Japandi with warm brass accents and smoked oak discipline
global_style_notes: Preserve exact joinery and layout from the floor plan; do not change window locations or major openings.
Derive all light direction from visible windows and placed practical fixtures only.
Maintain identical finish names across rooms: 'Smoked Oak' for wood joinery, 'Charcoal Slate' for main upholstery, 'Jet Black' for honed stone, 'Warm Brass' for metal accents.
Keep scale believable: low seating heights, compact desk, and human-scale pendant drops.
negative_prompt: no color inconsistency between rooms, no mismatched furniture across sightlines, blur, distort, low quality, warped architecture, flicker, unstable camera, unrealistic lighting, muddy shadows, layout drift

room_plans.lighting_instruction: Primary daylight from bay window (daylight warm ~5500K). Evening: low-output warm LED cove behind slatted panel and one floor lamp (warm 2200–2700K). Keep exposure stable; emphasize directional window pull with soft shadow edges.
room_plans.dalle_scene_details: Wide living shot, frontal 3/4 view toward bay window. Keep vertical lines true and furniture layout stable. Foreground: neutral woven area rug and centered black marble coffee table. Seating: reference shared object 'low-profile 3-seater sofa — charcoal slate linen upholstery (Charcoal Slate #2F2F2F), smoked oak tapered legs, low bench form'. Work corner at bay return: reference 'standing desk — compact adjustable-height standing desk, dark-stained oak top, matte black steel T-leg base' with 'open bookshelf — freestanding smoked oak 5-shelf unit, matte finish' placed perpendicular to sightline. Add bonsai plant on low stand near window (reference 'bonsai plant — small rounded ficus bonsai in unglazed ceramic pot'). Visible light sources: bay glazing and subtle cove backlight behind slat wall; no additional overheads. Materials: matte charcoal walls, smoked oak slats, low-pile warm oat rug, honed black marble.
room_plans.video_prompt: Very slow dolly in, camera moves forward from living-room edge toward bay window while light from bay window shifts gently, shadows lengthen subtly, consistent exposure, cinematic, photorealistic
must_include: low-profile 3-seater sofa — charcoal slate linen upholstery (Charcoal Slate #2F2F2F), smoked oak tapered legs, low bench form
black marble coffee table — round monolithic black marble, honed finish (Jet Black #0B0B0B)
neutral woven area rug — low-pile wool blend in warm oat
standing desk — compact adjustable-height standing desk, dark-stained oak top, matte black steel T-leg base
open bookshelf — freestanding smoked oak 5-shelf unit, matte finish
bonsai plant — small rounded ficus bonsai in unglazed ceramic pot
must_avoid: visible clutter on desk (exposed cables, many papers)
furniture or objects that break the line-of-sight proportions

room_plans.lighting_instruction: Daylight from bay corner window sculpts table surface. Single aged-brass dome pendant centered over table (warm 2200–2700K) provides evening key. Maintain consistent pendant finish and drop across scenes.
room_plans.dalle_scene_details: 3/4 angle from living-room perspective looking toward the bay dining corner so the dining table is visible within the larger open plan. Place 'rectangular smoked oak dining table — matte smoked oak finish (Smoked Oak #73503A)' under the pendant 'aged warm brass dome pendant — single centered over table, finish "aged warm brass" (Warm Brass #D99A48), interior white diffuser'. If rug continuity is beneficial, include 'neutral woven area rug — low-pile wool blend in warm oat' partially under table aligned with living area rug edge. Keep chairs slim and finished in smoked oak or dark metal to match table. Visible light sources: bay glazing and the single pendant only.
room_plans.video_prompt: Super smooth camera glides horizontally from right to left, slow truck across the open plan revealing the dining corner, directional sunlight moves across table surface, consistent exposure, cinematic, photorealistic
must_include: rectangular smoked oak dining table — matte smoked oak finish (Smoked Oak #73503A)
aged warm brass dome pendant — single centered over table, finish "aged warm brass" (Warm Brass #D99A48), interior white diffuser
neutral woven area rug — low-pile wool blend in warm oat
bonsai plant — small rounded ficus bonsai in unglazed ceramic pot
must_avoid: mismatched chair finishes visible from the living room

room_plans.lighting_instruction: Daylight from the small kitchen window (cooler tone relative to bay). Supplement with under-cabinet warm LED (2700–3000K) and one small aged-brass task pendant or sconce over prep. Do not introduce extra ceiling sources.
room_plans.dalle_scene_details: Counter-level view focusing on the visible prep run and open shelving. Use smoked oak open shelving (match 'Smoked Oak' finish) with minimal dishware and one small plant or ceramic. Visible finishes: matte dark stone countertop and matte cabinetry; if a pendant is present reference 'aged warm brass dome pendant — finish "aged warm brass" (Warm Brass #D99A48)' but scaled small for kitchen. Keep composition tight to avoid showing unmeasured parts of the plan. Visible sightlines to the dining table should include the exact 'rectangular smoked oak dining table' or pendant when framed.
room_plans.video_prompt: Super smooth camera drifts gently upward and sideways (aerial drift) revealing prep surfaces, subtle light shift from window and under-cabinet LEDs, consistent exposure, cinematic, photorealistic
must_include: smoked oak open shelving (matching Smoked Oak finish)
aged warm brass dome pendant — finish "aged warm brass" (Warm Brass #D99A48) if present
matte dark stone countertop (visible prep surface)
must_avoid: bright reflective stainless surfaces in the camera frame

room_plans.lighting_instruction: Window on top wall is the only natural source. Use low-warm bedside lamps (aged-brass finish), and a concealed warm LED strip behind the slatted headboard for evening ambience. Keep exposure restrained to preserve mood.
room_plans.dalle_scene_details: Tight-to-wide bedroom framing: low bed centered under single window with smoked oak vertical slat headboard wall. Use layered bedding (light gray sheets, charcoal duvet) and two sculptural brass bedside lamps (reference brass finish 'Warm Brass #D99A48'). Keep decorative objects minimal: one small ceramic and one plant. Maintain material continuity: smoked oak slats same finish as living/dining joinery. Visible sightline to bathroom should show consistent finishes but no duplicated furniture between bedroom and living beyond the shared slat language.
room_plans.video_prompt: Static slow push-in (static zoom) into bed vignette, camera holds and then tightens for detail on layered textiles and brass bedside lamp, consistent exposure, cinematic, photorealistic
must_include: vertical smoked oak slat accent (use same Smoked Oak finish as living joinery)
layered bedding in light gray and charcoal
sculptural brass dome bedside lamp (aged warm brass accent)
must_avoid: bright decorative mirrors that reflect light sources unnaturally

room_plans.lighting_instruction: Frosted window is the ambient source; add warm LED mirror strip or single aged-brass sconce for task light only. Keep overall light low and warm; no daylight from other rooms should be simulated.
room_plans.dalle_scene_details: Small bathroom frame focusing on vanity and frosted window. Use matte dark vanity top and warm LED mirror or single brass sconce (match 'Warm Brass' finish). Towels and accessories limited to warm neutrals. Keep tile and wall textures matte; do not show extraneous fixtures beyond the measured plan. Preserve verticals and believable scale.
room_plans.video_prompt: Static slow push-in (static zoom) into vanity detail, steady camera holds on material textures and warm mirror light, consistent exposure, cinematic, photorealistic
must_include: matte black or dark stone vanity top
warm LED mirror or brass sconce (aged warm brass accent)

shared_objects.appearance_spec: low-profile 3-seater sofa — charcoal slate linen upholstery (Charcoal Slate #2F2F2F), smoked oak tapered legs, low bench form, seat height 38 cm, no visible feet from side angle
black marble coffee table — round monolithic black marble, honed finish (Jet Black #0B0B0B), low profile, 90 cm diameter
neutral woven area rug — low-pile wool blend in warm oat (warm oat neutral), rectangular under living area ~8x5 ft, subtle linear weave, fringe-free
rectangular smoked oak dining table — matte smoked oak finish (Smoked Oak #73503A), compact 6-seat footprint (approx 150 cm length), slender square legs
aged warm brass dome pendant — single centered over table, aged warm brass finish (Warm Brass #D99A48), interior white diffuser, diameter approx 45 cm, recommended 60 cm drop above table
standing desk — compact adjustable-height standing desk, dark-stained oak top (Dark Oak/Smoked Oak family), matte black steel T-leg base, single monitor arm, integrated cable channel
open bookshelf — freestanding smoked oak 5-shelf unit, matte finish (Smoked Oak #73503A), vertical slat back, slim profile
bonsai plant — small rounded ficus bonsai in unglazed ceramic pot (soft stone color), placed on low stand

warnings: Ensure all shared object specs are used verbatim in visual generations to avoid cross-room mismatches.
Dining room is tight — verify chosen dining table scale in production so chairs do not block the bay glazing in camera moves.
Preserve vertical lines and window geometry exactly; do not push heavy perspective corrections that alter the floor plan.
